title: The South Addis Housing Wave: What First-Time Condo Buyers in Gelan Need to Plan for Moving Day
excerpt: South Addis housing wave: first-time condo buyers Gelan moving day checklist, handover keys, compound rules, budget tips for new homeowners.
content:
The South Addis housing wave: what first-time condo buyers in Gelan need to plan for moving day

First-time condo buyers in Gelan's South Addis wave face handover excitement plus moving-day reality: keys at 4 p.m., truck booked for 8 a.m., compound wants COI nobody mentioned. Planning separates proud homeowners from stressed couples on curb with loaded Isuzu and closed gate.

This guide lists what first-time Gelan condo buyers must plan before moving day.

Handover versus move-day gap

  • Snag list walk—photo every defect before furniture masks wall.
  • Keys, remotes, and parking card same envelope—verify count.
  • Meter numbers photographed for utility transfer.
  • Management move permit form—often separate from sales handover.
  • Elevator booking if mid-rise—not automatic with key receipt.

First-time buyer budget lines

Professional move quote; compound move deposit; elevator fee; curtain/blind install; deep clean pre-furniture; spare keys; basic toolkit; internet installation—not just sofa and bed.

What to move first

Kitchen box, bedding, toilet paper, shower curtain, phone charger, documents folder—first night survival before full unload marathon.

Common first-timer mistakes

Buying furniture before measuring elevator; booking truck before permit; assuming old home deposit timeline covers overlap rent; no food plan day one.

Active Movers first-home package

Handover-day flexible window; manifesto assist; basic furniture placement floor plan optional survey.

Conclusion

South Addis first-time condo buyers succeed moving day when handover paperwork, compound permit, and realistic budget align—housing wave reward is keys plus plan not keys plus panic.
author: Active Movers Team
